theme: buses on a loop line that bunch like beads
title: Beads on a Wire: Loop-Line Holding Control
strategies: never hold: let dwell-feedback run free (reproduces the evaluator's weak never-hold baseline)

target-schedule chasing: hold each bus back toward the nominal headway using only its OWN observed gap, with a fixed one-size-fits-all gain (ignores the instance's actual feedback strength and the coupling to the following bus)

two-way looking search: replicate the evaluator's own coupled-ring dynamics internally and grid-search a linear holding law that reacts to BOTH a bus's own gap and the gap of the bus behind it, trading off correction speed against capacity (hold) cost per instance
spectral placement: derive the holding gain that drives the ring's slowest-damped (antisymmetric, alternating) mode toward its fastest possible decay instead of only reacting locally
